decision_summary: The chip peak temperature (89.26°C) exceeds the limit (85.0°C). The most effective and conservative lever is to increase the thermal conductivity of the heat spreader to improve lateral heat distribution away from the hot spot. I will apply a moderate 1.2x increase, which is well within the allowed 2.0x step ratio and bounds.

changes.reason: Increasing spreader conductivity enhances lateral heat spreading, reducing the local temperature gradient under the chip and lowering the peak temperature.

expected_effects: Reduction in chip_max_temperature towards the 85.0°C constraint
More uniform temperature distribution across the heat spreader
risk_notes: Diminishing returns may occur if the bottleneck shifts to the base plate or boundary conditions rather than the spreader itself.
Ensure the new conductivity value does not exceed physical material limits in a real-world context, though it is permitted in this simulation range.
